div.p: your behalf in the name of the Lord,1 and this Society shall rejoice, and knowledge and intelligence shall flow down2 from this time henceforth;3 this is the beginning of better days to the poor4 and needy,5 who shall be made to rejoice6 and pour forth blessings on your heads.7

When you go home, never give a cross or unkind word to your husbands,8 but let kindness, charity and love crown your works henceforward; don’t envy the finery and fleeting show of sinners, for they are in a miserable situation; but as far as you can, have mercy on them, for in a short time God will destroy them, if they will not repent and turn unto him.9

Let your labors be mostly confined to those around you, in the circle of your own acquaintance, as far as knowledge is concerned, it may extend to all the world; but your administering should be confined to the circle of your immediate acquaintance, and more especially to the members of the Relief Society. Those ordained to preside10 over and lead you, are authorized to appoint the different officers, as the circumstances shall require.

If you have a matter to reveal, let it be in your own tongue;11 do not indulge too much in the exercise of the gift of tongues, or the devil will take advantage of the innocent and unwary. You may speak in tongues for your own comfort, but I lay this down for a rule, that if anything is taught by the gift of tongues, it is not to be received for doctrine.

President Smith then gave instruction respecting the propriety of females administering to the sick by the prayer of faith,12 the laying on of hands, or the anointing with oil;13 and said it was according to revelation that the sick should be nursed with herbs and mild food, and not by the hand of an enemy.14 Who are better qualified to administer than our faithful and zealous sisters, whose hearts are full of faith,15 tenderness, sympathy and compassion. No one. Said he was never placed in similar circumstances before, and never had given the same instruction; and closed his instructions by expressing his heartfelt satisfaction in improving this opportunity.
The Spirit of the Lord was poured out16 in a very powerful manner, never to be forgotten by those present on this interesting occasion. (April 28, 1842.) DHC 4:602–607.
